page.content: In the inventory channel analysis for ASML Holdings covering the period from 2019 to 2024, the year 2019 recorded units sold at 1,000,000 with revenue of $500M and a growth rate of not specified. In 2020, units sold increased to 1,200,000, generating revenue of $600M and a growth rate of 20%.

lines.text: In the inventory channel analysis for ASML Holdings covering the period from 2019 to 2024, the year 2019 recorded units sold
at 1,000,000 with revenue of $500M and a growth rate of not specified. In 2020, units sold increased to 1,200,000, generating
revenue of $600M and a growth rate of 20%.

page.content: The trend continued in 2021 with units sold reaching 1,500,000, resulting in revenue of $750M and a growth rate of 25%. In 2022, units sold rose to 1,800,000, yielding $900M in revenue and a growth rate of 20%. By 2023, units sold further increased to 2,000,000, generating revenue of $1B with an 11% growth rate, while the forecast for 2024 anticipates units sold at 2,200,000 and revenue at $1.1B, reflecting a growth rate of 10%. The total units sold over this period amounts to 10,200,000, with total revenue reaching $4.4B and an average growth calculated at not specified. The average units sold per year stands at 1,706,667, with an average revenue of $733.33M. The company holds a market share of 35% and maintains a production capacity of 2,500,000 units. ASML operates 15 branches and employs 5,000 individuals, with an R&D investment of $200M contributing to a net profit of $350M.
